features.name: Mechanical Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Must_be: audible alarm on activation is regulatory table-stakes. Safety (9) — 95 dB alarm directly serves E08 OS=16. Confidence (8): mechanical trigger cannot fail silently under power loss.

features.name: Key Override
features.rationale: Must_be: mandatory for operational use. Custodians and PE staff must access exits daily without triggering alarms. Skill (7) + Stress (7): reduces daily operational burden. Pain (7): eliminates false alarm disruption.

features.name: IoT Connectivity
features.rationale: One_dimensional: schools choosing wireless IoT over mechanical-only specifically for remote event capture. Pain (8): transforms security coordinator from patroller to dashboard monitor. Time (8): remote alert eliminates discovery-by-inspection delay.

features.name: Remote Monitoring Dashboard
features.rationale: Attractive: portfolio dashboard is genuinely unexpected in this procurement category. Stress (8): real-time certainty about all exit states. Pain (8): eliminates campus walk-around. Delight (8): administrators discover they can view all doors from a tablet.

features.name: Predictive Maintenance
features.rationale: Attractive: battery depletion is the dominant failure mode in schools (5-15 activations/day). Reliability (9): directly addresses E13 OS=15 and E14 OS=15. Confidence (8): coordinator knows no door is monitoring-dark.

features.name: Regulatory Compliance Documentation
features.rationale: Attractive: post-Uvalde legislation in 32+ US states makes auditable exit logs an explicit purchasing driver. Pain (9) + Time (9): eliminates manual log assembly before state inspections. Confidence (9): continuous documentation eliminates compliance audit findings.

features.name: Retrofit Capability
features.rationale: One_dimensional: all 49-year-average-age school buildings lack conduit to secondary exits. Cost (9): eliminates electrician labor; 50-door school saves ~$30,000-50,000 vs. wired. Skill (9): facilities crew installs without electrical license.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of wireless monitoring coverage gaps in reinforced concrete school buildings where 868 MHz signal attenuation reduces transmission reliability
Minimize the likelihood of undetected alarm monitoring failure at high-traffic exits where daily activation frequency exhausts a 9V battery before the 2-year maintenance target
Minimize the time required to schedule and execute battery replacement across all wireless exit alarm units distributed across a multi-building school campus
Minimize the integration effort required to relay EasyWave alarm events into district-standard access control or BMS platforms standardized on BACnet, KNX, or IP-based protocols
Minimize the likelihood of deliberate removal or displacement of surface-mounted exit alarm housing units by unsupervised student populations
